steps.title: Use the distance prediction
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that languages farther from Africa retained fewer sounds from the original language. Evidence supports it if a region closer to Africa has more sounds than a region farther away.

steps.title: Reject opposite patterns
steps.content: Choice A gives more sounds to the farther region. Choice C gives fewer sounds to the closer region. Choice D compares regions at about the same distance, so it does not test the distance pattern. The remaining finding follows the predicted closer-more, farther-fewer pattern. The answer is B.
